algorithms.title: Bag-Mask Ventilation Technique
algorithms.description: Steps for effective positive-pressure ventilation with a bag-mask device.
steps: Position at the patient's head and open the airway (head-tilt/chin-lift, or jaw-thrust if spinal injury is suspected).
Select an appropriately sized mask that covers the mouth and nose without covering the eyes or extending past the chin.
Insert an airway adjunct if indicated (OPA if no gag reflex; NPA if a gag reflex may be present).
Apply the mask using the E-C clamp: thumb and index finger form a 'C' sealing the mask, the other fingers form an 'E' lifting the jaw.
Squeeze the bag smoothly over about 1 second, delivering only enough volume to see the chest rise.
Match the rate to context: 2 breaths per 30 compressions (no advanced airway), or 1 breath every 6 seconds (rescue breathing or advanced airway).
If the chest does not rise, reposition the airway, improve the seal, and check for obstruction before re-attempting.
